questions.q: What was the name of the sage Vishwamitra when he ruled as a king, before he took up penance?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra was originally the warrior-king Kaushika before he renounced his throne for asceticism.

questions.q: Into what role was Vishwamitra born before becoming a sage?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra was born a Kshatriya, a warrior-king, before attaining sagehood through penance.

questions.q: With which rival sage did Vishwamitra have a famous long-standing rivalry?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra's defining rivalry was with the sage Vasishtha, foremost of the Saptarishi.

questions.q: According to tradition, which sacred mantra is Vishwamitra revered as the seer (rishi) of?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra is revered as the seer of the Gayatri mantra (Rigveda 3.62.10), addressed to the solar deity Savitr.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which two royal brothers did Vishwamitra take with him to protect his sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra led the young princes Rama and Lakshmana from Ayodhya to guard his sacrificial rites.

questions.q: Which demoness did Vishwamitra guide the young Rama to slay in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Under Vishwamitra's guidance, Rama slew the demoness Tataka who terrorized the forest.

questions.q: What was the wish-granting cow named whose dispute set Vishwamitra against Vasishtha?
questions.explanation: The dispute centered on the divine cow at Vasishtha's hermitage, named Nandini, whom the king Kaushika coveted.

questions.q: What is the highest sage-rank that Vishwamitra ultimately attained through penance?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra rose through the ranks of austerity to finally attain the title of Brahmarshi.

questions.q: In which epic does Vishwamitra serve as the guru leading Rama and Lakshmana on their first adventures?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra's role as the boy princes' preceptor is narrated in Valmiki's Ramayana.

questions.q: Why did the king Kaushika first turn from his throne toward severe penance, according to tradition?
options: To win a war against demons
After his clash with the sage Vasishtha over the cow
questions.explanation: His humbling clash with Vasishtha over the divine cow convinced Kaushika to pursue spiritual power instead.

questions.q: Which celestial nymph (apsara) is said to have disrupted Vishwamitra's penance and borne him a daughter?
questions.explanation: The apsara Menaka broke Vishwamitra's austerities and became the mother of his daughter Shakuntala.

questions.q: Which famous heroine, later central to Kalidasa's drama, is described as the daughter of Vishwamitra and Menaka?
questions.explanation: Shakuntala, heroine of Kalidasa's play, was born to Vishwamitra and the apsara Menaka.

questions.q: Which king did Vishwamitra famously try to send to heaven in his bodily form, creating a new heaven for him?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra used his ascetic power to send King Trishanku heavenward and began fashioning a parallel heaven for him.

questions.q: When the gods halted King Trishanku's ascent, what did Vishwamitra create in defiance?
options: A second set of stars and a southern heaven
questions.explanation: In his resolve Vishwamitra began fashioning a new set of constellations and a southern heaven for Trishanku.

questions.q: Into which group of revered sages is Vishwamitra counted in the traditional lists of the Saptarishi?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra is honored as one of the Saptarishi, the seven great seers, in the traditional lists.

questions.q: Which Veda preserves the largest body of hymns attributed to Vishwamitra and his lineage?
questions.explanation: The third Mandala of the Rigveda is traditionally attributed to Vishwamitra and his descendants.

questions.q: When Vishwamitra escorted Rama to King Janaka's court, whose marriage did the journey help bring about?
options: The marriage of Bharata and Mandavi
The marriage of Lakshmana and Urmila
The marriage of Rama and Sita
The marriage of Krishna and Rukmini
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra escorted Rama to Mithila, where Rama won Sita by breaking Shiva's bow.

questions.q: Which two sacred invocations did Vishwamitra teach the young Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra imparted the mantras Bala and Atibala to Rama, granting him strength and freedom from fatigue.

questions.q: Whose sacrificial rite did Vishwamitra take Rama and Lakshmana into the forest to protect?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra brought Rama and Lakshmana specifically to guard his own yajna from marauding demons.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which demon, son of Tataka, attacked Vishwamitra's sacrifice and was hurled away by Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama struck Maricha, Tataka's son, with an arrow that flung him far across the sea, defending Vishwamitra's rite.

questions.q: To which dynastic lineage did Vishwamitra belong as a king before his renunciation?
questions.explanation: As King Kaushika he belonged to the Kushika line, from which his patronymic Kaushika derives.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, whom did Rama free from a curse on the journey with Vishwamitra, restoring the wife of sage Gautama?
questions.explanation: On the journey with Vishwamitra, Rama's arrival liberated Ahalya from the curse of sage Gautama.

questions.q: Which power of Vasishtha did Vishwamitra, as King Kaushika, fail to overcome, leading him to seek brahmanical power?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha's Brahmadanda absorbed all of Kaushika's missiles, convincing him that ascetic power surpassed weapons.

questions.q: Which sage finally addressed Vishwamitra as 'Brahmarshi', confirming the title he had long striven for?
questions.explanation: It was his old rival Vasishtha who at last hailed Vishwamitra as a Brahmarshi, ending their feud.

questions.q: In the tale of King Harishchandra, how did Vishwamitra test the king's truthfulness?
options: He cursed Harishchandra to lose his sight
He turned Harishchandra into a deer
He demanded Harishchandra's entire kingdom and wealth as a gift
He gifted Harishchandra a celestial chariot
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra tested Harishchandra by claiming his kingdom and wealth, reducing him to poverty to prove the king's adherence to truth.

questions.q: When the apsara Rambha was sent to disturb Vishwamitra's penance, what did the sage do to her, according to tradition?
options: Sent her back to Indra's heaven
Cursed her to become stone for a long age
questions.explanation: Tradition relates that Vishwamitra cursed Rambha to remain a stone for a long age, then resumed his penance with greater control.

questions.q: In the Aitareya Brahmana, what did Vishwamitra do for the youth Shunahshepa after he was freed from being sacrificed?
options: He adopted Shunahshepa as his own son
He sent Shunahshepa to Indra's court as a minister
He turned Shunahshepa into a star
questions.explanation: In the Aitareya Brahmana account, Vishwamitra accepted Shunahshepa as a son after the youth was saved through hymns to the gods.

questions.q: The Gayatri mantra of which Vishwamitra is the seer is set in which Vedic poetic meter?
questions.explanation: The mantra is set in the Gayatri meter of twenty-four syllables, from which it takes its name.

questions.q: Which celestial bow, kept at King Janaka's court, did Vishwamitra encourage Rama to attempt to lift and string?
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra urged Rama to lift Shiva's mighty bow Pinaka, which Rama broke while stringing it, winning Sita.

questions.q: Vishwamitra's life is often cited in the tradition as the prime example of which spiritual idea?
options: That birth alone fixes one's spiritual rank forever
That weapons are superior to austerity
That kingship is the highest worldly goal
That a Kshatriya could attain Brahmarshi-hood through penance
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra exemplifies the principle that intense penance, not birth alone, could raise a king to the rank of Brahmarshi.
